description: Resizable split layout built on react-resizable-panels. ResizableHandle renders a PanelResizeHandle, which under the hood is a focusable <div> with role="separator" and aria-valuenow reflecting the panel size. The library handles keyboard resize via arrow keys automatically.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to / from the resize handle.
On a horizontal handle, decreases / increases the left panel's size by a step.
On a vertical handle, decreases / increases the top panel's size by a step.
Collapses / expands the panel to its minimum / maximum (when collapsible).
Toggles collapsed state for collapsible panels.

focus.notes: Handle uses focus-visible:ring-1 ring-ring with ring-offset-1. Panels themselves are not focusable; only the handle between them.

announcements: Each handle announces as a separator with its current aria-valuenow (panel size percentage).
The library updates aria-valuenow as the user resizes, so AT can read the live size.
screenReader.notes: When withHandle is true, a visible grip icon (GripVertical) is rendered inside the handle; it's decorative (pointer-events-none implicitly via the SVG). The grip does not change the accessible name.

contrast.ui: Handle uses bg-border; grip container adds a subtle border. Both meet AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Size changes apply instantly (no transition by default). Consumers who add their own animation should gate it on prefers-reduced-motion.

consumerResponsibilities: Set min / max / default sizes on each ResizablePanel so the layout can't collapse into unusable states.
Provide an accessible label on ResizableHandle via aria-label (e.g. "Resize sidebar"); the default has no descriptive name.
For layouts where resize isn't critical, consider using a non-resizable grid instead; keyboard resize is a niche interaction.
